title: Refugee Family Journey
themeCategory: Refugee Journey - Family Escape and Rebuilding

emotionalArc.struggle: Execution order / War begins - family must flee immediately
emotionalArc.perseverance: Dangerous journey through multiple countries, border crossings, hunger, fear
emotionalArc.breakthrough: Kindness from strangers, reaching safety, children starting school, family rebuilding together

emotionalMoments.description: Mother spoke out for women's rights, Taliban issued execution warrant
From that point on, I stopped being a child

emotionalMoments.moment: Hussein playing clown to distract family
emotionalMoments.description: Trying to make things seem normal during horror

emotionalMoments.moment: Father carrying sick son across Calais jungle
emotionalMoments.description: Time was running out, carrying son on back to save his life

emotionalMoments.moment: Robbed and starving, no food for days
emotionalMoments.description: We were robbed twice and had no food for days
I'd gone from being 'Hamed Amiri' to a boy called 'John', and later, 'a refugee'
First time in a long time we felt at home
They treated my brother—a 14-year-old non-national who couldn't speak English—with nothing but compassion
At 5:00 am my husband woke me up and said that it's time to leave the country
We just took our kids and went from the city. Took documents, kids, nothing else
We had to spend six or seven days at the border. We ran out of food, we ran out of water and I had panic attacks for the first time in my life
Polish people spent nights at the railway station and they gave their personal apartments to families. It was like, you can have tears in your eyes
At the last minute when we went to the car, our dog looked into our eyes, and we cried a lot
In April 2022, their home back in Ukraine was destroyed
They go to the store and every week buy me flowers. It's like a tradition from Ukraine

keyQuotes: For me, this isn't a refugee story; it's a family story. It's a love story—three boys with a love of football, a parent who wants to sacrifice everything for their kids.
If we didn't leave home, my mum would not be around. If we didn't leave home, my brother wouldn't have lived as long as he did.
What does 'refugee' mean to me? It wasn't a word that I chose, it was a word that was given to me. I'm just Hamed. I'm a human being.
I don't want it to be a sad story, I don't want it to be a pity story—I want it to be a story of hope.
Don't be afraid to start something new, to start a new life.

characterNames.note: Will use generic names - check character-names-tracker.json to avoid repetition
